subject: Geography
title: Antarctic Mining Ban
description: A short lesson to help you apply Antarctic Mining Ban.

hook.text: The Madrid Protocol bans all mining in Antarctica until at least 2048.

buildup.text: Antarctica holds coal, iron, and potentially oil—but the treaty puts science over extraction.

discovery.text: The ban has kept the continent pristine—the cleanest air and water on Earth are measured there.

twist.text: When the ban comes up for review, resource-hungry nations may push to lift it.

climax.text: Whether Antarctica remains protected depends on whether cooperation survives rising resource pressure.

punchline.text: Antarctica is safe—for now. The clock runs out in 2048.

quiz.question: Until when is mining banned in Antarctica?
